candidates.why: Revenue component, but only populated in some years and excludes services/construction.
Revenue component, but appears largely unpopulated and would not capture total top-line alone.
Revenue component, but only a small subcomponent in limited years.
Direct total top-line revenue/sales line and matches Net sales values.
Not revenue; wrong concept.
Disposal gain, not operating revenue.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit total top-line revenue row.

candidates.why: Best match for direct operating input costs / cost of sales.
Semantically correct label but appears unused here; 'Materials and services' is the populated primary cost line.
Balancing row, not an operating cost line.

variables.notes: Chosen as the main direct cost line analogous to cost of goods sold.

candidates.why: Operating overhead personnel cost; good SG&A component.
Operating overhead bucket; good SG&A component and populated.
Balance-sheet item, not income statement expense row for SG&A mapping.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles
candidates.why: Amortization should be excluded from SG&A per rules.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A subtotal exists, so selected the best operating overhead components. Excluded depreciation/amortization and totals. Did not add Stock-based Compensation separately to avoid likely double counting within operating expenses/personnel costs.
No dedicated research and development expense line is visible on the income statement.

candidates.why: Broad finance expense line; usable fallback if no cleaner interest line is available.
Interest-related but too narrow, lease-specific only.
Duplicate/broader finance expense label; likely less useful than explicit interest wording.
Best available reported line containing interest expense, though includes other finance costs.
Most semantically exact label if populated in some years; suitable highest-priority option.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Too narrow and supplemental, lease-interest only.

variables.notes: Priority list uses the most semantically exact interest line first, then broader finance-cost labels as fallbacks. Excluded lease-only supplemental rows from final choice because they are too narrow.

candidates.row_label: Share of profits accounted for using the equity method
candidates.why: Income statement earnings line, not book equity.

candidates.row_label: Investments accounted for using the equity method
candidates.why: Asset investment line, not shareholders' equity.
Only a component of equity, not total owner equity.

candidates.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the parent company
candidates.why: Best match for book equity excluding non-controlling interests.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to the non-controlling interest
candidates.why: This is minority interest, not parent book equity.
Includes minority interest when present, so inferior to owner-only equity.

final_choice.row_label: Total equity attributable to owners of the parent company

variables.notes: Preferred owner/parent equity line to exclude non-controlling interests and avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement profit allocation, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement allocation line, not balance sheet minority equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to the non-controlling interest
candidates.why: Direct balance sheet minority/non-controlling interest equity line.
Also a direct balance sheet minority interest label; good fallback.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Income statement OCI allocation, not minority equity on balance sheet.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to the non-controlling interest

variables.notes: Priority list of balance-sheet minority interest labels. These represent non-controlling interests and complement BE without overlap.
